sn35.63:1.2: Then Venerable Migajāla went to the *Bhagavā* …
sn35.63:1.3: Seated to one side, Venerable Migajāla said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn35.63:1.4: “‘One who dwells alone, one who dwells alone,’ bhante, it is said.
sn35.63:1.5: To what extent, bhante, does one dwell alone, and to what extent does one dwell with a second?”
sn35.63:2.1: “There are, Migajāla, forms cognizable by the eye, desirable, lovely, agreeable, appealing, tied to sensuality, enticing.
sn35.63:2.10: then for him, as he delights in it, welcomes it, and remains holding to it, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.11: When delight is present, there is passion;
sn35.63:2.12: when passion is present, there is bondage.
sn35.63:2.13: Migajāla, a *bhikkhu* bound by the fetter of delight is called one who dwells with a second.
sn35.63:2.14: And a *bhikkhu* dwelling in this way, Migajāla, even though he resorts to remote lodgings in wilderness and forest, quiet, free from noise, solitary, away from people, suitable for retreat,
sn35.63:2.15: is still called one who dwells with a second.
sn35.63:2.17: Because *taṇhā* is his second, and it has not been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:2.18: Therefore he is called ‘one who dwells with a second.’
sn35.63:2.2: If a *bhikkhu* delights in that, welcomes it, and remains holding to it,
sn35.63:2.3: then for him, as he delights in it, welcomes it, and remains holding to it, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.4: When delight is present, there is passion;
sn35.63:2.5: when passion is present, there is bondage.
sn35.63:2.6: Migajāla, a *bhikkhu* bound by the fetter of delight is called one who dwells with a second.
sn35.63:2.8: There are, Migajāla, tastes cognizable by the tongue, desirable, lovely, agreeable, appealing, tied to sensuality, enticing.
sn35.63:2.9: If a *bhikkhu* delights in that, welcomes it, and remains holding to it,
sn35.63:3.1: And there are, Migajāla, forms cognizable by the eye, desirable, lovely, agreeable, appealing, tied to sensuality, enticing.
sn35.63:3.10: then for him, as he does not delight in it, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it, delight ceases.
sn35.63:3.11: When delight is absent, there is no passion;
sn35.63:3.12: when passion is absent, there is no bondage.
sn35.63:3.13: Migajāla, a *bhikkhu* detached from the fetter of delight is called one who dwells alone.
sn35.63:3.14: And a *bhikkhu* dwelling in this way, Migajāla, even though he lives near a village, crowded with *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, male lay followers, female lay followers, kings, royal ministers, sectarians, and disciples of sectarians,
sn35.63:3.15: is still called one who dwells alone.
sn35.63:3.17: Because *taṇhā* is his second, and it has been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:3.18: Therefore he is called ‘one who dwells alone.’”
sn35.63:3.2: If a *bhikkhu* does not delight in that, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it,
sn35.63:3.3: then for him, as he does not delight in it, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it, delight ceases.
sn35.63:3.4: When delight is absent, there is no passion;
sn35.63:3.5: when passion is absent, there is no bondage.
sn35.63:3.6: Migajāla, a *bhikkhu* detached from the fetter of delight is called one who dwells alone …
sn35.63:3.7: and there are, Migajāla, tastes cognizable by the tongue …
sn35.63:3.8: and there are, Migajāla, *dhammas* cognizable by the mind, desirable, lovely, agreeable, appealing, tied to sensuality, enticing.
sn35.63:3.9: If a *bhikkhu* does not delight in that, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it,
